search.query:
| makeresults count=12 | streamstats count AS row | eval order=row-1 | eval type=case( row=1, "markdown", row=2, "description", row=3, "freetext", row=4, "dropdown", row=5, "radio", row=6, "checkbox", row=7, "date", row=8, "number", row=9, "description", row=10, "spl", row=11, "dropdown", row=12, "freetext" ) | eval question=case( row=1, "# Incident Triage Form
**Complete all fields** to ensure accurate tracking.

- Use the most specific answer available
- Add context in Notes when unsure
- Review the [Splunk docs](https://docs.splunk.com/) if you need field guidance", row=2, "How this example works", row=3, "Affected hostname?", row=4, "Severity level", row=5, "Confirmed malicious?", row=6, "Attack techniques observed", row=7, "Date first observed", row=8, "Number of affected systems", row=9, "Reference detection SPL", row=10, "Containment SPL run", row=11, "Assigned analyst", row=12, "Notes" ) | eval options=case( row=2, "This standalone description row uses the **question** column as the heading and the **options** column as the body. It supports inline *italic*, **bold**, and [links](https://www.splunk.com/).", row=4, "low,medium,high,critical", row=5, "yes,no,unknown", row=6, "phishing,malware,lateral movement,data exfil,ransomware,other", row=9, "index=security sourcetype=wineventlog EventCode=4625 | stats count by src_ip user | where count > 5 | sort - count", row=11, "analyst1,analyst2,analyst3,unassigned", true(), "" ) | eval description=case( row=3, "Use the hostname exactly as it appears in your SIEM.

Examples:
- `WIN-DC01`
- `linux-web-02`", row=4, "Select the highest applicable severity based on **business impact**.", row=5, "Choose `unknown` when the evidence is still inconclusive.", row=6, "Select all that apply. Checkbox answers are saved as a comma-separated list.", row=7, "Use the first timestamp where suspicious activity was seen.", row=8, "Approximate count is fine if exact impact is unknown.", row=9, "Read-only description-style SPL reference. The options column holds the SPL; Form Viz renders it as formatted Splunk code.", row=10, "Multi-line SPL input. Paste the SPL you ran to investigate or contain this incident, then use **Format SPL** if you want to tidy it before submitting.", row=11, "Pick the current owner for follow-up.", row=12, "Add extra context, assumptions, or links to supporting evidence.", true(), "" ) | table options order question type description | append [ | makeresults | eval options="triage_form", order="", question="Form Name", type="form_name", description="" | table options order question type description ] | append [ | makeresults | eval options="form_responses", order="", question="Output Lookup", type="form_kv_output", description="" | table options order question type description ]

html.p: This dashboard generates its example rows inline so every supported field type is visible even if a local form_config lookup has been customised or cached. The shipped form_config lookup contains the same examples for reuse.
The example lookup demonstrates every supported input type. The SPL reference uses a description row formatted as Splunk code, and the SPL answer uses type=spl for a multiline input.
Use the description column for markdown helper text under a question label and above the input. Optional order controls render order (ascending). Without it, SPL row order is used.
On submit, responses are written to the form_responses KV Store with user, form_name, and order fields for filtering and analysis.

search.query: | search type!="form_name" type!="form_kv_output" | table options order question type description
| table options order question type description
| inputlookup form_responses | search form_name="triage_form" | sort -_time +order | table timestamp, submission_id, user, form_name, order, question, answer
